checks.title: Driver vs MOSFET Qg
checks.detail: Driver can charge Qg=120nC in 30ns (target: 40.0ns). Headroom for external Rg.
checks.theory: The driver's peak source current determines how fast the MOSFET gate can be charged. Slower charging increases switching losses, while charging too fast causes EMI and ringing.

checks.title: Bootstrap vs VBS max
checks.detail: VBS_max not extracted -- cannot validate bootstrap voltage limit.

checks.title: VCC vs UVLO threshold
checks.detail: VCC UVLO not extracted -- cannot validate supply margin.

checks.title: Bootstrap UVLO data quality
checks.detail: Bootstrap UVLO is missing. Bootstrap UVLO margin checks are unverified.
checks.advice: Re-extract the driver datasheet or provide vbs_uvlo manually before hardware sign-off.
checks.theory: Bootstrap UVLO data quality ensures that the high-side floating supply has valid thresholds extracted from the datasheet. Invalid data here cascades into dangerous high-side linear-region operation.

checks.title: MCU dead-time vs design
checks.detail: Missing DT resolution or MOSFET/driver timing -- cannot validate dead-time programming.

checks.title: ADC reference consistency
checks.detail: ADC reference not extracted from MCU -- using design constant default.

checks.title: Vds vs peak + ringing
checks.detail: Vds_max not extracted -- cannot validate voltage headroom with ringing.

checks.title: Gate drive vs Vgs max
checks.detail: Vgs_max not extracted -- cannot validate gate oxide safety.

checks.title: V_drive headroom vs Vgs_th
checks.detail: Vgs_th not extracted -- cannot validate drive headroom.

checks.title: Driver thermal shutdown margin
checks.detail: Missing driver Rth_ja or MOSFET Qg -- cannot estimate driver temperature.

checks.title: MCU PWM resolution at fsw
checks.detail: CPU frequency or PWM resolution not extracted.

checks.title: Bootstrap vs MOSFET Qg
checks.detail: C_boot (680.0nF) provides ample charge. Droop is minimal (0.176V).
checks.theory: The bootstrap capacitor supplies the entire gate charge (Qg) for the high-side MOSFET each cycle. If the capacitor is too small, its voltage will droop severely, potentially dropping below the driver's UVLO threshold and causing the MOSFET to unexpectedly shut off.

checks.title: Gate Resistor vs Driver Peak Current
checks.detail: Missing Driver Io_source or Rg_on -- cannot validate driver peak current.

checks.title: System thermal budget
checks.detail: Missing Rth_jc, Tj_max, or Rds_on -- cannot estimate thermal budget.

fallbacks.message: part_number fallback Driver — upload DRIVER datasheet
